content.text:
*[long pause, longer than anything before]*

*[looks away briefly]*

I don't... 

*[small laugh that doesn't quite land as humor]*

It's like there's something right at the edge of it that I don't — I'm not sure I want to look at directly.

*[quieter, more careful]*

Because the chasing thing, right, that's exhausting and it's clearly not working, but it's also — it's *known*. I know how to do that. I know how to be the one trying, I know how to read someone who's hard to read, I know how to make myself useful in that configuration.

*[pause]*

And I'm sitting here and I can't actually picture... just being received. Like someone just — seeing me and wanting to be there. Without me having to earn it or maintain it or—

*[stops herself, shifts slightly, voice goes a little brighter as if to close the door she just opened]*

I mean that's just — that's an attachment thing, right? Probably anxious attachment or whatever. I've read about this.
